response: convert the initial tax liability begins with calculating the art collection tax, which is $2 million multiplied by a reduced rate of 19% after applying the 5% discount to the original 20% rate, resulting in $380,000. convert the real estate tax is determined by applying the progressive brackets to the $5 million value, yielding $100,000 for the first million, $300,000 for the next two million, and $400,000 for the remaining two million, totaling $800,000. convert the securities portfolio tax is computed by subtracting the $100,000 charitable deduction from the $3 million value to get a taxable base of $2.9 million, then applying the 15% rate to arrive at $435,000. convert the sum of these three components gives an initial total estate tax liability of $1,615,000. convert the scenario changes when the real estate valuation is corrected by increasing the $5 million figure by 10% to reflect the actual market value of $5.5 million. convert this new valuation shifts the progressive tax calculation so the first $1 million is taxed at 10%, the next $2 million at 15%, and the remaining $2.5 million at 20%, producing a new real estate tax of $950,000. convert the art and securities taxes remain unchanged at $380,000 and $435,000 respectively under the new conditions. convert the final recalculated total tax liability for the entire estate is now $1,765,000.
